_comment: Canonical solver-binary manifest. Consumed by dexbtx_miner.solver_updater (binary) and dexbtx_miner.wrapper_updater (the top-level `version` field) on every miner startup. To add a new platform: add an entry under `platforms`. To force-upgrade across a fork boundary: set `min_required_sha256` on the relevant platform entry — a host whose binary != min that cannot download the new one will REFUSE to mine. v1 single-binary schema (top-level sha256+url) is still recognized by the updater as legacy (treated as x86_64-linux); the v2 platforms-dict schema below is preferred.
notes: v0.4.18 — SOLVER AUTO-HEAL (wrapper-only; SOLVER BINARY UNCHANGED from v0.4.17 = 70f16afd / btx-prebuilds-v0.32.11-preempt; purely additive). The long-running solver daemon can latch a bad runtime state around a pool restart's job discontinuity — emitting gross-wrong V3 digests (100% code-23) or hanging — and a pool reconnect does NOT clear it (the daemon persists across stratum reconnects), so the rig mines 0 valid shares until the *process* restarts. Proven on home-1070: a full miner restart revived it where pool reconnects + a pool-side socket kick did not. The wrapper now self-heals: a watchdog bounces the solver daemon (next slice respawns a fresh one) on either (B) >=8 consecutive submitted-and-rejected shares with zero accepts since the last accept [resets on any accept; ramp-up/slow/high-diff safe], or (A) no solver result for 90s while the process is alive [the hang case; a/r/b freezes so B can't fire]. Escalating cooldown prevents restart loops; resets on the next accept. All thresholds in config.heal_*. PRIOR — v0.4.17 — GPU SATURATION + tip-preempt. Headline: ~1.5-2x nonce throughput on most NVIDIA cards. Root cause: the miner fed the GPU in slices too short (5s) for the driver to ramp to its high P-state (~12s on a 1070), so the card idled between slices at ~half throughput. Fix is config-only on the wrapper side — nonces_per_slice 20M->100B and solver_max_seconds_per_slice 5s->30s — so each solver call is ONE long continuous solve that holds the high clock the whole time. Validated on home-1070 (GTX 1070 / sm_61): P5/696MHz/~700 N/s -> P2/~1800MHz/~120-150W/~1500 N/s, 0 rejects over hours. Tip-preempt makes the long slice safe: a real new-block tip change aborts the in-flight slice within ~ms via SIGUSR1 (the v0.4.17 solver advertises {preempt:true} in its daemon_ready line; the wrapper only sends SIGUSR1 to a preempt-capable solver, so a half-upgraded fleet is safe), instead of burning up to ~17% of a cycle on a dead tip; a stale-share guard drops any wrong-parent shares. SOLVER = btx v0.32.11 source + the dexbtx SIGUSR1 patch (consensus-IDENTICAL to v0.32.11, KAT-verified byte-for-byte), published as btx-prebuilds-v0.32.11-preempt. min_required_sha256 left at the v0.32.11 binaries: pre-v0.32.11 (broken/regression) solvers are still force-upgraded; v0.32.11 hosts pick up the preempt solver via NORMAL auto-update (not forced — no refuse-to-mine risk). x86_64 is one STATIC-cudart fat binary (CUDA 12 AND 13), native cubins sm_61;sm_75;sm_80;sm_86;sm_89;sm_90;sm_120, LTO, built on home-1070. aarch64 cuda12 (sm_80;90;120) + cuda13 (sm_80;90;120;121) via build-solver-aarch64.yml. arm64-darwin Metal solver carried forward from v0.32.10 (gets the wrapper saturation config; no preempt — capability gate keeps it safe).

x86_64-linux.force_upgrade_reason: Floor stays at the v0.32.11 build (first consensus-correct MatMul-V3 solver). Pre-v0.32.10 solvers produce 100% invalid shares after the fork at mainnet block 130,500; the v0.32.10 build fell back to slow CPU per-nonce. Hosts below this floor that cannot download the new binary refuse to mine. The v0.4.17 target (70f16afd) adds SIGUSR1 tip-preempt and is consensus-identical to v0.32.11; v0.32.11 hosts upgrade to it via normal auto-update (not forced).
x86_64-linux.notes: btx v0.32.11 + dexbtx SIGUSR1 tip-preempt patch (daemon_ready advertises preempt:true). STATIC cudart (driver-only; runs on CUDA 12 and 13). CUDA 12.8 toolkit, native cubins sm_61;sm_75;sm_80;sm_86;sm_89;sm_90;sm_120 (GTX 10-series through RTX 50-series). LTO. Built on home-1070; KAT-verified byte-identical CPU digest to btxd consensus (ci-refvec).

aarch64-linux.force_upgrade_reason: Floor at the v0.32.11 build (first consensus-correct MatMul-V3 solver). Pre-v0.32.10 solvers produce 100% invalid shares after the fork; the v0.32.10 build fell to slow CPU per-nonce. The v0.4.17 target adds SIGUSR1 tip-preempt, consensus-identical to v0.32.11; v0.32.11 hosts upgrade via normal auto-update.
aarch64-linux.notes: btx v0.32.11 + SIGUSR1 tip-preempt. CUDA 12.8, archs sm_80;sm_90;sm_120 (Ampere through Blackwell consumer). Default ARM entry. CUDA-13-only hosts (GB10/Spark, sm_121) should set DEXBTX_PLATFORM_KEY=aarch64-linux-cuda13. Built via build-solver-aarch64.yml.

aarch64-linux-cuda12.force_upgrade_reason: Floor at the v0.32.11 build. The v0.4.17 target adds SIGUSR1 tip-preempt, consensus-identical to v0.32.11; v0.32.11 hosts upgrade via normal auto-update.
aarch64-linux-cuda12.notes: Opt-in via DEXBTX_PLATFORM_KEY=aarch64-linux-cuda12. btx v0.32.11 + SIGUSR1 tip-preempt. CUDA 12.8 build, archs sm_80;sm_90;sm_120 (no sm_121 — use cuda13 build for GB10/Spark). Built via build-solver-aarch64.yml.

aarch64-linux-cuda13.force_upgrade_reason: Floor at the v0.32.11 build. The v0.4.17 target adds SIGUSR1 tip-preempt, consensus-identical to v0.32.11; v0.32.11 hosts upgrade via normal auto-update.
aarch64-linux-cuda13.notes: Opt-in via DEXBTX_PLATFORM_KEY=aarch64-linux-cuda13. btx v0.32.11 + SIGUSR1 tip-preempt. CUDA 13.0, archs sm_80;sm_90;sm_120;sm_121. Covers GB10/Spark + RTX Blackwell consumer needing a CUDA-13 runtime. Built via build-solver-aarch64.yml.

arm64-darwin.force_upgrade_reason: MatMul-V3 fork at mainnet block 130,500 — pre-v0.32.10 solvers produce 100% invalid shares after the fork.
arm64-darwin.notes: Apple Silicon Metal backend, carried forward unchanged from BTX v0.32.10 (no SIGUSR1 preempt; the v0.4.17 wrapper's capability gate keeps it safe — it gets the saturation config but never receives a preempt signal). Re-hosted under the v0.32.11-preempt tag so install.sh's single PREBUILDS_TAG resolves for every platform. CI verified cpu_digest == metal_digest == reference_digest before original publish.
